transcript.text: Hi, Mariana, sorry I'm late.
Hello, no problem.
How are you?
Good, I was just in another interview for like an operations sort of program manager role I have, but we started late, so then we went late.
So here I am.
Sure, no problem, don't totally understand.
Anyway, it's nice to meet you, remind me where you're based, where you live.
Rio de Janeiro, Brazil.
Oh, nice, okay, cool.
I was there last summer.
Really?
Did you enjoy it?
I loved it.
I could live in Rio for sure.
loved it.
Did you like the beach?
The weather was nice?
Yeah, well, it was actually August, or yeah, July, so it was actually a bit colder, but I loved it.
Like,
We did hiking, obviously, and I like running, so I was just running all the way down the beach, we went to a friend's wedding in Búzios, I think it was called.
Oh my god, I love it, it's my favorite town in that region, because it's kind of far from Rio downtown.
Yeah, it was so nice, like, there was beautiful little swimming beaches there, it was, because it was kind of cold, it was July though, so it wasn't warm.
So, that's the thing, that's a problem we have, it really doesn't matter what time of the year it is, the water will be cold in Rio de Janeiro specifically.
In other parts of Brazil, like other states that have beaches, they have warm waters, but we don't, like every single part of the year.
In summer, yes, it's a bit warmer than in July, for example, but it is too cold.
But anyways, it's so hot.
But then the cold water, it's good, you know?
Yeah, totally.
Well, I'm excited to meet you.
And I was just looking back at your resume.
But I'll give you a little background on me and then the company.
And then I'd love to hear what you've been doing for the last few years.
So I live in Vancouver, by the way, in Canada.
And we're a very remote company.
But I joined in January.
So I've been here just three months.
And then my team is all of operations.
So recruiting people, finance, IT, legal, essentially, like all the company operations.
And then the business.
So the company is really new.
It's less than a year old.
We have now 40 customers, some pretty big brands.
And the company is doing very well revenue-wise.
It's grown super fast.
And so the team has also grown super fast.
We're already 50 people.
And when I joined in January, I think we were 15.
So really fast growing.
And basically, the company today, the service we provide today, I say today because it's going to change, but today we use AI workflows we've developed to produce content.

So, for example, we would have a new customer and we would use the AI workflows to research the company, the competitors, maybe who their users are, things about their product, and we're prompting the workflows and the LLMs to write, produce content.
And then we have lots of, the reason the team's so big is because we have humans like prompting, checking, quality checking, sometimes writing themselves, sometimes editing.
So it's very much like human paired with AI.
So over time, we hope that the AI product gets better.
And then it's something we could even show in.
in a UI, like in a product itself, in a platform.
Today, it's like all behind the scenes and our team does it.
And then we are just like an agency to the customer versus like in the future, we'd love for the customers to be logging into something.
Maybe they can do some stuff.
So eventually to more of like a product company.
Okay, there will always be the need for like humans, though, obviously, because AI is still like light years away from where it needs to be.
So the team is going to keep growing, for sure.
But anyway, so the need for these roles.
So basically, it's just very crazy in a good way.
But it's just, it's been crazy.
Like, we have a lot of new customers, we have a lot of new people on the team.
We have a lot of prospects who want to sign contracts.
So everyone on the leadership team is just very underwater with a lot of stuff to do.
So I'm what I'm doing is
I'm trying to find different assistants for, like, essentially each org of the company.
So we have, like, sales, we have marketing, we have operations, and we have the tech org.
And then, like, obviously the CEO as well is also looking for an EA.
So anyway, I've been working with Nier, like, not for long.
We just started working together last week, but they helped me meet you.
So they're just looking for different folks.
So anyway, I'll pause there if you have any questions.
Otherwise, we can jump into your background.
Okay, no questions.
So let's get going.
Cool.
So I've been working with a bunch of stuff over the last few years.
Also, my educational background is a bit diverse.
I studied arts.
Now I study marketing, but I have also studied translation and so on and so forth.
And for the last couple of years, I've been...
Working with a Lithuanian company called Bored Panda.
I started with them as a proofreader.
So checking text produced by AI, especially by ChatTBG, to see if it was sounding human and so on.
This is an entertainment and media company.
So normally we check the text that is going to enter on a video for YouTube, Facebook, and so on and so forth.
And after one year with them, was promoted to a virtual assistant.
And this name is just because they didn't have a better name.
Because what we do is we use AI to generate the audio.
So it's the second part of the process.
First the text, then the audio, and then we have the final video.
And we use for this, right now we are using two, Eleven Labs and Lovo AI, mostly Eleven, then Lovo.
And I also check the rest of the workflow.
If the translator...
Editors are delivering their files if the editors are getting the files that they need and so on.
And for that, we use Monday.com to streamline the whole workflow and the whole production flow.
And so as a virtual assistant, besides this specific tool, Eleven Labs, we use a lot ChatGPT.
And I personally, since I am very privileged because I am in both positions, both the text and this second voice part, I understand a bit better the workflow than my colleagues.
So I've developed two things to help me and the others, like make things progress more smoothly.
So first of all, I developed a specific ChatGPT AI bot to help the translators have all of their files in the same format so that when we get these files,
We don't spend time putting everything in the correct format, so then we can start working.
And by doing this, we saved around 10% of time, me and the other virtual assistants.
And that was the start, and I liked doing this thing of using AI and doing things.
And then I moved on to a more complex project and the voice editing thing, because now I am working with videos that are 10 minutes, 20 minutes, 30 minutes.
And for that, I use a specific part in Eleven Labs that is called Studio, in which I can have chapters, I can assign specific voices to different characters, and so on.
This is more complex, and then, again, the text is also more complex.
But then I found one problem.
The translators weren't checking the text with the video.
And then I started checking...
To go over the data, like what are the workflows?

I started to ask the project managers and the other virtual assistants if they were finding the same problem and what was going on, what happened, why this text that the translators receive is different from the video.

And then I discovered that because the text that the translators get is the original one that was written by a scriptwriter, and when it goes to the studio, the director and the actors change it because they feel it's not aligning, it's not very good, etc.
And then the translators weren't watching the video and reading the text, which for me was like, oh my god, this is not something that can happen because I have a translator and...
How come they weren't doing it?
They didn't know or they weren't?
Exactly.
So they were just drinking.
accountability.
Exactly, yes.
Yes, they...
Just reading and doing it.

They didn't open the video, the project manager didn't check, but I checked because I need to see if this is a question, if they are laughing, if they are crying, so that I can generate the voice according to the video.
And then I was like, okay, if I only tell them to check, I don't know if they are going to check.
This is not going to help at all.
So I decided to develop a style guide for them.
used Canva for this.
I designed everything.
I created the text.
I put examples, screenshots of what happens, what's possible.
I detailed all the workflows, both the studio and mine and their workflows, because again, I am in a privileged position.
I understand how all of the things happen and they don't.
And if they knew how AI works, they would know, like, what to, what one.
to use and so on, because this AI that we use specifically is very unpredictable.
Sometimes, for example, I had a problem like this.
I was generating audio for Norwegian, and the text was correct.
It was in Norwegian, but the AI decided that it didn't like Norwegian.
So it generated everything in Swedish.
And then it was like we had to change and go to global AI because the text was okay.
The problem really was the AI.
So I talked to the HR and my project manager, and I was like, guys, I created this, and I want to show the translators what we do.
Because I think that when they understand that their job will also get easier, and automatically all of the other people's jobs will get easier, and we will get a better product at the end.
So I led this training session.
Jan!
here on Zoom with the translators.
It was around 30 people, probably 15 nationalities.
And I showed them our guide and I showed them the actual AI.
generated audios with them on the call so they could see how it worked and so on.
Because AI is very new and especially for us in localization, in translation, it's even newer.
It's been one year that we have been doing these things.
And then this was a few weeks ago, almost a month ago, and now I am starting to see results.
My work time has reduced to 50% because 50% of my time was checking the text with the video.
Now that the translators are doing this, my work is flowing much better and much faster and all of the other virtual assistants as well.
And we have been delivering things, I was going to say.
In Portuguese, sorry.
Faster and like with more quality in the end.
So deadlines are very important in this kind of job because we have specific days that we can post on YouTube to generate more viewers and more things like that.
So our deadlines are much more comfortable now that we have developed this whole system and so on and so forth.
And I thank them, the company, a lot for allowing me this flexibility of actually creating things and putting things into action.
So now it's getting, it sounds like it's a lot better, but do you have to wait until it hits, their work hits you to know if they did it or not?
Or is there any other way?
Is somebody, you mentioned program manager, like is someone else holding them accountable?
That's I was going to say.
Yes.
So after this, two things are happening.
The project managers are aware that we had.
And I also check a little bit because I just want to make sure, you know, but even though the project managers are checking, it's much faster than it was before.
And when they do check, I get a message on monday.com saying you can skip chapter two.
You can skip chapter four and so on because like this one didn't make it to the video in the end.
And I know that they checked because of this.
They give me a heads up and then the project goes on and flows smoothly, you know, because the thing for me was really how do I make sure that they are going to follow?
Because if I only create a file, a document, they can read and whatever, you know.
So I decided to do this training.
decided training know that community
I really wanted them to not only to follow, but to understand the process.
For me as a translator, it helped a lot when I started doing the second step of the process, because then my translating job got better.
I was able to think like, OK, the AI will not be able to read these words.
The AI will get confused if I use these words and so on and so forth.
So, but I didn't have like, nobody was, do you want to see how it works?
Nobody gave me the opportunity.
So I was like, OK, I am going to give the translators the opportunity so that we can try to make things flow better.
And I am a freelancer for this company.
So at the same time, I have a full time job as an executive assistant for a New York company called Flax Hospitality.
They, it's one company, but they have a bunch of.
of.
Companies inside it.
So they are in the hospitality business.
They have two restaurants, two shops that sell like food and things like that.
They have a winery and they produce the wine.
They have a farm and so on.
And they have a catering company as well.
And in this job, I do, I like to separate my job into three groups.
So the first one is the administrative part.
I am the assistant to both of the CEOs of the company.
They are married.
It's a man and a woman.
And I manage their schedules.
I manage their meetings.
I manage their email.
They don't know their email anymore because my email is connected to theirs.
So I open everything.
I read.
I answer what I can.
And when I can't answer something, I make to-do lists for them to take a look at this.
They gave, they give me a heads up or they give me instructions.
And I go and answer.
And we organize everything using Basecamp most of the times.
But we also talk on, call on the phone, and we also have chat messages and so on.
Besides that, I have the marketing part of the job.
So they have Instagram and Facebook accounts for all of those companies.
I am the one who manages and who creates the content for all of this.
So I use Canva most of the time.
And I also, so I post, I schedule, I come up with the content.
I have a full autonomy on the marketing part.
I post what I think is good, what I think works.
I keep track of what days work better for the posting, what days generate more views, more comments, and so on.
And then I schedule according to this.
I create the things with Canva.
The only thing I really need for this is the pictures, because I am not there.
So I communicate with the rest of the team, the people that...
are in the restaurants and that are working the events, etc.
I tell them a few guidelines of what I would like.
They take pictures, send me, and I post.
And I also take care of the customer interactions.
So the comments, the messages, and so on.
Everything of the social media, it's me, basically.
I am the social media person.
And the third part would be the operations.
So I would say that my job is essentially taking things off the table so that they, my two bosses, can be on site.
Because it's a very physical job, like being at the farm, being at the restaurant, and talking to guests, and so on.
So I take care of all of the other things that I can, so that they can do the things that only they can do.
And then this involves becoming a bridge between them and the rest of the team.
So I
I assist our accountant a lot, for example, I pull invoices from specific systems, I get also invoices on the email, and I upload them to our financial system, which is R365.
It's focused on hospitality businesses.
I maintain financial records, I help with the tips division between the team using Excel and Google Sheets and so on.
And I also, on the email, I also take care of the customers.
So I talk to the catering clients, for example.
I create quotes for them using our system, Toast.
I answer their questions.
Sometimes they want to know if they can arrive two hours early to set up the event.
And since we have restaurants, sometimes they have trouble with the reservations.
Going to our conversations that they forgot to say when they actually booked for the first time, so I insert this, and so on and so forth.
And I would say that there's a fourth part, but it's very small.
I am also there public relations.
So my boss, Christopher, he has won some awards already.
He's a sommelier.
He has won some awards like this.
They have had some recognitions for the restaurants as well.
So what happens is that they get invitations for interviews and for featuring in newspaper articles and magazine pieces and so on.
So I also deal with the journalists.
I send them the info, pictures, and so on and so forth.
And, well, that's it.
And right now, something that I am doing is...
Let's get
I have recently been studying a bit about automations, mostly on YouTube, watching some tutorials and stuff, because I am very curious.
And I discovered this one called Airtable that does one thing that I do manually.
So I created a sheet with our clients that I have to follow up, especially the catering clients, because Marmarie sends the menu.
And I let them take some weeks to think, and then I follow up to see if they are interested.
And Airtable does that.
So I can write a message, I can automate the day that it's going to send, and how many days or how many weeks it's going to send.
So I am working to implement this, because my bosses, they are open on the content parts, the social media parts.
They are very open, they allow me to do whatever I want.
But in terms of...
These operation things, they are still a bit close-minded, and I am trying to convince them that it's going to work better for everyone.
It's going to save us loads of time, and it's going to prevent errors, right?
Because it's going to be something that it's going to go straight to one specific person, and so on and so forth.
So that's it.
In the past, I have been a translator for entertainment companies, so dubbing and subtitling, mostly Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, and HBO Max, but some others as well.
Again, deadlines all the time, especially because when we deal with a TV show, a movie, a cartoon, we are dealing with millions of dollars, so a deadline can't be missed at all.
Like, it's not a possibility.
So I try to keep myself organized by creating to-do lists and creating sheets with all...
So I can visualize them all together and see if I can take that project or not.
And also, we deal a lot with confidentiality.
I signed NDAs, and I can't really talk about projects before they are on.
So when they actually premiere on Netflix or whatever.
And besides that, was, before that, sorry, I was also an ESL teacher.
Always dealing with English.
So I taught kids, teenagers, and adults for almost five years.
And before that, again, executive assistant, but now in fashion.
I was the executive assistant to the CEO of the company.
And I did the assistant things of scheduling meetings and stuff.
But in this one specifically, I was also in charge of the, of buying things.
So I bought the fabric, I bought the trims, I was in close contact with all the suppliers, both in Brazil and internationally, and so on and so forth.
If you have any questions, I know it's a lot.
And I have another meeting in two minutes.
guess my one main question is like, what are you?
It sounds like you have lots of responsibilities right now.
Why are you looking for something new?
Because I would like to have one more responsibility.
Even more?
Yes, no, like, because I am a freelancer, and I have a full time job, because both of them don't pay enough.
So I would like to leave both of them, exactly, and combine everything in one thing.
And then what else?
So possibility of growth, because in both of the companies I am right now, that's not that possible.
And I wouldn't like to be an assistant my whole life, you know?
Yeah, I was going to ask, like, what's your dream?
I don't know, even if it's just five years from now, like, what kind of jobs are you most looking?
So from all of the things that I do, the two things I like the most are operations and marketing.
And I think that in marketing, in this company specifically, I have a lot of autonomy, but I am only one person.
So in the future, I would like to lead a team and actually present them with guidelines and so on, like I did in this experience.
Exactly.
Because it's like I've been a teacher, so I really like helping people and guiding people and talking a lot and so on.
So I think that in the future, that's what I would like to do.
Awesome.
Okay, well, I do have another call, so I need to go, but it was so nice meeting you.
And thank you for walking me through everything.
And I will, usually I just speak with Anna in the air, so we'll probably get back to you soon on the next steps.
Okay, thank you very much.
It was a pleasure to meet you.
Yeah, nice to meet you.
Bye.
Thanks.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory interview between Bridget McGillivray and Mariana Marins for a potential assistant role at a rapidly growing AI-driven content production company.](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Company: Less than 1 year old, 40 customers, 50 employees (up from 15 in January), AI+human content production](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=758.0)
  - [Mariana: Diverse background (arts, marketing, translation), currently juggling 2 roles (VA at Bored Panda, EA at Flax Hospitality)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=941.0)
  - [Impressive skills: AI workflow optimization, training leadership, social media management, operations](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1094.0)
  - [Seeking: Single full-time role combining responsibilities, growth potential, team leadership (ops/marketing focus)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=2075.0)

## Topics

### Company Overview

  - [Less than 1 year old, rapidly growing AI-driven content production company](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=758.0)
  - [40 customers (including big brands), strong revenue growth](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=761.0)
  - [50 employees (up from 15 in January when Bridget joined)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=774.0)
  - [Current: AI workflows + human QA/editing for content creation](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=782.0)
  - [Future: Develop UI/platform for customer self-service](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=836.0)
  - [Hiring assistants for each org: sales, marketing, operations, tech, CEO](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)

### Mariana's Background & Current Roles

  - [Education: Arts, marketing, translation studies](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=946.0)
  - [Bored Panda (Lithuanian entertainment/media company):](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
      - [Started as AI-generated text proofreader](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=964.0)
      - [Promoted to "Virtual Assistant" (AI audio generation, workflow management)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=986.0)
      - [Developed ChatGPT bot for translation formatting (10% time savings)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1088.0)
      - [Created style guide & led training for 30 translators (50% time reduction)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1316.0)
  - [Flax Hospitality (NY-based multi-business hospitality company):](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1548.0)
      - [Full-time EA to co-CEOs](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1589.0)
      - [Manages admin, marketing, operations across multiple businesses](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1709.0)
      - [Social media management, customer interaction, financial tasks](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1692.0)
      - [PR responsibilities (awards, media inquiries)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1824.0)

### Mariana's Skills & Achievements

  - [AI workflow optimization & training leadership](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1094.0)
  - [Social media content creation & management (full autonomy)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1639.0)
  - [Operations bridge between leadership and team](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1733.0)
  - [Financial record-keeping, invoicing, tips division](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1740.0)
  - [Customer service (catering, reservations)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1778.0)
  - [Exploring automation tools (e.g., Airtable for follow-ups)](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1868.0)
  - [Previous experience: Translator (Netflix, Amazon, HBO), ESL teacher, EA in fashion industry](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=1943.0)

### Career Aspirations

  - [Seeking single full-time role to replace current juggling act](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=2075.0)
  - [Desires growth potential beyond assistant roles](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=2082.0)
  - [Interested in operations and marketing leadership](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=2101.0)
  - [Aims to lead and guide teams, leveraging teaching background](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=2126.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Bridget to discuss with Anna from Nier about potential next steps](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=2144.0)
  - [Follow-up communication to be sent to Mariana regarding interview process](https://fathom.video/share/o8YS4sPjvanJ68xYbr4ak2cZpvzNyLq2?tab=summary&timestamp=2154.0)
